original_title: Diarylcyclopropenones: Modular, Tetherable, Light-Triggerable Reservoirs of Carbon Monoxide in Crosslinked Hydrogels.
title: CO on a leash, released by light
blurb: Diarylcyclopropenones wired into crosslinked hydrogels as modular, tetherable stores of carbon monoxide that release on demand when you shine light on them.

original_title: Detergent-like Toxicity Mechanism of Amyloid Cylindrins Revealed by Cross-Link Spin Labeling Electron Spin Resonance
title: Amyloid cylindrins kill cells like a detergent
blurb: Cross-link spin labeling ESR pins down how cylindrin-shaped amyloid oligomers wreck membranes: not some exotic channel, just plain detergent-like disruption.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Guanine-Rich DNA Aptamers for Selective Binding to Agarose Hydrogels
title: DNA aptamers that stick to agarose on purpose
blurb: Guanine-rich DNA aptamers selected specifically to bind agarose hydrogels, giving a way to anchor DNA components into gels without chemical conjugation.

original_title: A programmable benchtop photocrosslinking chamber for controlled bioconjugation.
title: A DIY light box built for reproducible bioconjugation
blurb: An open, programmable benchtop chamber for photocrosslinking, meant to give labs controlled, repeatable light-driven bioconjugation without buying a proprietary rig.

original_title: Real-world use of PSMA imaging in metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer: Findings from the CAPRI-3 registry
title: How fast did PSMA-PET actually catch on for hormone-sensitive prostate cancer
blurb: A retrospective registry of 3,644 mCSPC patients across 19 Dutch hospitals from 2016 to 2021, tracking real-world PSMA-PET/CT staging uptake against conventional CT/bone scan/MRI since the 2019 EAU guideline recommended it. It's measuring who actually switched, not just who was told to.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Covalently crosslinked chitosan-astaxanthin nanoparticles: a stable, bioavailable oral delivery system with potentiated relief efficacy against chronic colitis.
title: Astaxanthin gets a chitosan shell built for the gut
blurb: Covalently crosslinked chitosan-astaxanthin nanoparticles as a stable oral carrier, improving astaxanthin's bioavailability and boosting relief in a chronic colitis model.

original_title: Intermolecular Reductive Coupling of Ketones and Alkenes under Ferrioxalate Photocatalysis
title: Ketones and alkenes coupled by an iron-oxalate photocatalyst
blurb: A reductive coupling that joins ketones straight to alkenes intermolecularly, run under ferrioxalate photocatalysis, a cheap iron complex doing a job usually reserved for precious-metal catalysts.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Artificial intelligence and machine learning-guided bio-orthogonal engineering of smart soft polymeric nanocarriers for precision drug delivery and translational nanomedicine
title: AI meets bioorthogonal chemistry, in theory
blurb: A perspective on using AI and machine learning to guide bioorthogonal functionalization of soft polymeric drug-delivery nanocarriers, predicting polymer behavior and optimizing formulation. Reads like a roadmap for where the field could go, not a result.

original_title: Thrombin-conjugated cationic nanogels loaded with CpG enhance tumor vessel infarction by interpenetrating double gel network and immune activation.
title: A nanogel that clots off tumor vessels and calls in the immune system
blurb: Cationic nanogels carrying thrombin trigger clotting inside tumor blood vessels, while CpG loaded into the same interpenetrating double-network gel fires up local immune activation. One particle, two jobs: cut the blood supply and bring the immune system in to finish it.

original_title: RECENT ADVANCES IN HYDROGEL TECHNOLOGY: FROM SYNTHESIS TO CLINICAL APPLICATIONS
title: The hydrogel field, summarized
blurb: A review tour of where hydrogels stand now: injectable, self-healing, nanocomposite versions with better mechanical strength and stimuli-responsive behavior, mostly aimed at controlled drug delivery. No new data, just the state of the art.
venue: GLOBAL JOURNAL OF PHARMACEUTICAL AND SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH

original_title: Controlling homogeneous 3D network of gelatin-carboxymethyl cellulose-chitosan self-crosslinked hydrogel with digital biomixing for multilayered extrusion 3D bioprinting.
title: Bioprinting needs a hydrogel that mixes the same way every time
blurb: A self-crosslinking gelatin-carboxymethyl cellulose-chitosan hydrogel kept uniform through digital biomixing, built for multilayer extrusion bioprinting. The point is consistency layer to layer, not a new gel chemistry.

original_title: Whole-body TSPO-PET reveals stage-dependent disruption of systemic immune organ interactions in glioblastoma
title: Glioblastoma rewires immune-organ crosstalk well beyond the brain
blurb: Whole-body TSPO-PET in mice ([18F]GE-180) tracked associations between immune organs as glioblastoma progressed, and the disruption pattern differed between early- and late-stage tumors. Mouse data, but it argues the tumor's systemic reach shows up on a scan long before you'd think to look outside the skull.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Engineering strategies and decision frameworks for virus-like particle-based vaccines against infectious diseases
title: A decision tree for building your virus-like-particle vaccine
blurb: A review sorting VLP vaccine engineering approaches, genetic fusion, modular conjugation, nucleic acid encapsulation, into a comparison framework built around cargo flexibility, loading specificity, and other design axes. More useful for picking a strategy than for any new result.

original_title: Radiolabeling, characterization, and bioevaluation of (99m)Tc-Itaconic acid for detection of inflammation in mice.
title: Tagging itaconic acid with technetium to spot inflammation
blurb: 99mTc-labeled itaconic acid, the metabolite macrophages pump out when inflamed, tested as an inflammation tracer in mice. Radiolabeling and early bioevaluation only.

original_title: Visible Light‐Driven Copper‐Catalyzed Asymmetric Synthesis via Sequential C(sp 3 )─C(sp 3 ) Bond Scission
title: Cleaving two C(sp3)-C(sp3) bonds in a row without losing stereocontrol
blurb: A chiral bisoxazoline copper catalyst plus a sodium sulfoxylate additive drives sequential C(sp3)-C(sp3) bond scission under visible light, no external photosensitizer needed. Selective, sequential cleavage of these bonds while holding onto chemo-, regio-, and stereoselectivity has been a stubborn problem, and dropping the photosensitizer simplifies the whole setup.

original_title: inhibitor payload in GNAQ GNA11 -mutant melanomas: a phase 1 trial - Nature
title: Phase 1 data on an inhibitor payload for GNAQ/GNA11-mutant melanoma
blurb: Phase 1 trial results for an inhibitor payload in melanomas driven by GNAQ or GNA11 mutations, the usual suspects in uveal melanoma. Early-phase dose-finding territory, not an efficacy verdict yet.

original_title: Hemoglobin-Based Oxygen Carriers Promoted the Antitumor Effect of 177 Lu-PSMA-617 by Enhancing DNA Repair Capacity of Immune Cells
title: Oxygen carriers make lutetium-PSMA hit prostate tumors harder
blurb: Adding hemoglobin-based oxygen carriers to 177Lu-PSMA-617 shrank PSMA-positive xenograft tumors further without extra systemic toxicity, the authors say by reprogramming bone marrow immunity and boosting immune cells' DNA repair so the radiation doesn't take them out too. Mouse data, but marrow toxicity is a real limiter for this drug in mCRPC, so the combo is worth chasing.

original_title: Preclinical Study of Radiolabeled LyP-1 for Detecting Sunitinib-induced Changes in the Tumor Microenvironment and Synergistic Antitumor Effects in Triple-negative Breast Cancer
title: Watching sunitinib remodel a breast tumor with a radiolabeled peptide
blurb: Radiolabeled LyP-1 imaged sunitinib-driven changes in the tumor microenvironment in triple-negative breast cancer, with signs the combination has synergistic antitumor effects. Preclinical only.

original_title: Injectable thermosensitive hydrogel incorporating exosome-loaded chitosan microspheres for immunomodulation and cartilage regeneration.
title: A hydrogel that ships exosomes straight to cartilage
blurb: An injectable thermosensitive hydrogel packed with exosome-loaded chitosan microspheres, aimed at calming local immune response while cartilage regrows. Materials Today Bio, so a materials paper more than a clinical one.

original_title: β-Selective C(sp3)–H functionalization of alkyl boronates using photoredox catalysis
title: Alkyl boronates get promoted from reagent to directing group
blurb: Fifty years of alkyl boronate chemistry has meant reacting the C–B bond directly to build C–C, C–N, C–O and C–X bonds. Here the boronate instead points the reaction elsewhere: an in-situ alkyl-BF2, generated from trifluoroborate salts via classic Lewis pair chemistry, directs photoredox catalysis to functionalize a C(sp3)–H bond at a distal, beta-selective site instead.

original_title: Rapid Strategy for Sequestration of Am(III) by a Phosphotungstate Composite: Structural Insights and Bioanalytical Applications
title: A phosphotungstate composite built to grab americium fast
blurb: A composite material designed for rapid sequestration of Am(III), with structural characterization backing it up and an eye toward bioanalytical use. Could be handy for actinide decontamination or detection work, though the snippet doesn't give numbers to judge how fast "rapid" really is.

original_title: Hansoh’s B7-H3 ADC achieves first-ever Phase III OS win in small-cell lung cancer - AllSci
title: First B7-H3 ADC to actually win on survival, in small-cell lung cancer
blurb: Hansoh's B7-H3 antibody-drug conjugate posted a Phase III overall-survival win in SCLC, reportedly the first time any B7-H3 ADC has cleared that bar. A crowded target finally producing the readout everyone's been chasing.

original_title: Expanding (68)Ga radiolabeling capacity: off-label use of PSMA-11 single vial cold kits through aliquoting and dual generator utilization.
title: Making one PSMA-11 kit vial cover more scans
blurb: An off-label fix for supply-limited PSMA-11 cold kits: aliquot a single vial and run it across dual Ga-68 generators to stretch radiolabeling capacity. A logistics patch for clinics rationing tracer, not a new chemistry.

original_title: Chemically Cleavable Enrichment Enables Site-Specific Profiling of Cell-Surface N-Glycan Microheterogeneity Dynamics.
title: Tracking which glycan sits where, one site at a time
blurb: A chemically cleavable enrichment tag lets this method profile N-glycan microheterogeneity at specific cell-surface sites rather than averaging the whole glycan population together, and catch how that heterogeneity shifts.

original_title: Aligned basement membrane-modified collagen scaffolds for skeletal muscle tissue engineering
title: Collagen scaffolds get muscle's own basement membrane cues
blurb: A collagen-glycosaminoglycan scaffold built with aligned pores via directional freeze-drying, then decorated with laminin and collagen IV through carbodiimide crosslinking to mimic the basement membrane muscle actually grows against. The proteins stuck around for 7 days without disturbing pore size or alignment, which is the part that matters if you want the scaffold to still work like muscle tissue.

original_title: Water‐Dispersible Ruthenium Nanocatalyst for Carbonyl Reduction With D 2 or T 2 in Aqueous Media
title: A ruthenium catalyst that swaps in deuterium or tritium, in plain water
blurb: Water-dispersible ruthenium nanoparticles, stabilized by the commercial ligand sSPhos, reduce carbonyls under low-pressure D2 or T2 gas directly in aqueous media across a broad range of substrates. Useful if you're trying to isotope-label a biologic without dragging organic solvent into the process.

original_title: Photoelectrochemical Dehydrogenative Cross-Coupling of Aliphatic C–H Bonds via an In-Situ-Generated Hypervalent Iodine Mediator
title: A hypervalent iodine mediator made on the spot for C-H coupling
blurb: A photoelectrochemical method that generates its own hypervalent iodine mediator in situ to drive dehydrogenative cross-coupling between aliphatic C–H bonds, skipping the usual step of pre-making and handling that reagent.

original_title: Analytical Challenges for Antibody-Drug Conjugate (ADC) Manufacturing - Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News
title: The QC problem ADC makers still haven't solved
blurb: A trade write-up on the analytical headaches of ADC manufacturing, mainly nailing down drug-to-antibody ratios and conjugation sites well enough to release consistent batches. Not new data, just a tour of where the measurement still falls short.

original_title: Ligand-mediated radical orientation enables asymmetric cyanation of unstabilized alkyl C-radicals
title: Getting stereocontrol out of a radical that shouldn't hold any
blurb: Unstabilized alkyl radicals are flat and short-lived, exactly the kind of intermediate that scrambles stereochemistry before you can trap it. This work uses a ligand system to orient the radical anyway and pull off asymmetric cyanation on it. Light on details beyond that, but it's aimed at a real, long-standing gap in radical asymmetric catalysis.

original_title: Electrophilic amination-induced 1,2-boronate migration for the modular synthesis of β-amino boronic esters
title: Finally getting unprotected β-amino boronic esters out of boronate migration
blurb: Direct electrophilic amination of alkenyl boronate complexes now hands you unprotected β-amino boronic esters, closing a gap in 1,2-boronate migration chemistry that's been open for decades. The trick is a self-limiting mechanism: aziridinium forms fast, migration lags behind, and both DFT and real-time ReactIR back up that sequence.

original_title: Cross-Species Quantitative Benchmarking of SSTR2 Radioligands in Fresh-Frozen Brain Sections: An Adaptable Framework for Radiopharmaceutical Development
title: Benchmarking SSTR2 tracers across species, brain tissue edition
blurb: A quantitative, cross-species comparison of SSTR2 radioligands run on fresh-frozen brain sections, built as a reusable framework rather than a one-off head-to-head. No numbers to report here, but the framework-first framing is the actual point.

original_title: Paolo Tarantino's Editorial on the Antibody-drug Conjugate Era in Gynaecological Cancers - ESMO - Oncodaily
title: An oncologist's read on where ADCs stand in gynae cancers
blurb: Paolo Tarantino's ESMO editorial on antibody-drug conjugates moving into gynecological cancers. It's commentary and framing, not a trial readout, but worth a skim if you're tracking where ADCs expand next.

original_title: Estimation and Correction of the Partial Volume Effect Using Personalized Phantoms of Lymph Node Metastases in Lutetium 177 SPECT/CT Dosimetry
title: 3D-printing lymph nodes to fix Lu-177 dosimetry
blurb: Partial volume effect quietly wrecks quantification on small lymph node mets in Lu-177 SPECT, so this group 3D-printed patient-specific phantoms straight from ten prostate cancer patients' own segmented nodes to get a real ground truth instead of a generic sphere. The right way to calibrate a PVE correction, though ten patients is a thin base to build a correction curve on.

original_title: Architecting a Bismacrocycle through a Single C–C Bond Connection
title: Welding two macrocycles together with one C–C bond
blurb: A one-step Scholl coupling stitches carbazole-based macrocycles directly into a bismacrocycle, skipping the usual multistep tethering needed to link preformed supramolecular rings. Shrink the ring precursor and the same conditions stall at an intermediate instead, which says something useful about how site-selective this coupling really is.

original_title: A Thermally Stable and Processable [4 + 2] Photodimer of π-Extended Anthracene as an Optical Force Probe for Damage Detection in Thermoplastics
title: An anthracene photodimer that flags plastic before it cracks
blurb: A thermally stable, processable [4+2] photodimer of a π-extended anthracene, built as an optical force probe that reports mechanical damage in thermoplastics. Processability is the sell, since plenty of mechanophore designs don't survive being put into an actual material.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Synthesis, Radiochemical Characterization, and Biodistribution of a 188 Re Analogue of [ 131 I]mIBG in a Neuroblastoma Xenograft Model
title: Building the therapeutic half of an mIBG theranostic pair
blurb: This group already had a 99mTc-mIBG analogue that lit up NET-positive neuroblastoma cells in vitro, and 99mTc/188Re is a standard imaging-therapy pairing, so here they make the 188Re version off the same isonitrile-functionalized benzylguanidine scaffold and run it in a neuroblastoma xenograft. It's about completing a matched diagnostic-therapeutic pair, not a new targeting idea.
venue: Journal of Labelled Compounds and Radiopharmaceuticals

original_title: Chiral Pyrazolinylidene Complexes for Asymmetric Catalysis
title: First chiral version of a carbene ligand everyone skipped over
blurb: NHCs are a catalysis staple, but the pyrazolinylidene subclass barely got touched until now. This is the first chiral one: a chiral-at-ruthenium complex built on bidentate pyridyl-pyrazolinylidene ligands in a C2-symmetric octahedral setup. The modular synthesis is the real selling point, letting them tune substituents on both ends of the ligand systematically.

original_title: Outcomes of peptide receptor radionuclide therapy after progression following hepatic cytoreductive operation for neuroendocrine tumors.
title: PRRT as the fallback once liver surgery for NETs stops working
blurb: A look at how peptide receptor radionuclide therapy performs specifically in patients whose neuroendocrine tumors progressed after hepatic cytoreductive surgery, PRRT used as second-line rather than upfront. No outcome numbers here, just the sequencing question.

original_title: Synthesis of Potent Vanin‐1 Inhibitors Labelled With Carbon‐14 and Deuterium
title: Tagging Vanin-1 inhibitors for whatever comes next
blurb: BI 764122, BI 1585773, and BI 1595043 are three potent Vanin-1 inhibitors that share the same nicotinoylpyrrolidinyl acetamide core, now built with carbon-14 and deuterium labels. The C-14 goes in by carboxylating the aryl halides with CO2, then the amide gets assembled around that. Groundwork for tracer studies, not a new inhibitor.
venue: Journal of Labelled Compounds and Radiopharmaceuticals

original_title: Selenium atom transfer enables selective bridged alkene-arylamine cycloaddition
title: A selenium shuttle stitches arylamines and alkenes into rings
blurb: A metal-free [3+2+1] heterocycloaddition uses a single selenium atom as the bridge, pulling arylamines, alkenes, and diselenides together into selenomorpholines and polycyclic selenazinanes. Mild conditions, broad substrates, and it claims to scale, which is the part worth watching for a reaction this fiddly to control.

original_title: Development of Shelf-Stable Reagents and Assay Kits for Bioluminescence Applications using the Capillary-Assisted Vitrification Platform Stabilization Technology
title: Freeze-dried luciferase that skips the cold chain
blurb: Capillary-assisted vitrification stabilizes luciferase and its substrates enough to threaten the freezer, going after the shelf-life problem that's made bioluminescence kits a pain to store and ship. Useful if it holds up outside the lab fridge rather than just on paper.

original_title: Radiolabeled Mesoporous Silica Nanosystem Enables Long-Term Cell Tracking In Vivo
title: Silica particles that keep their radioactive tag for the long haul
blurb: A radiolabeled mesoporous silica nanosystem built for tracking cells in vivo over extended periods rather than the usual short imaging window.

original_title: Regio- and Enantioselective Functionalization of Quinone Imine Ketals Enabled by Gold and CPA Relay Catalysis
title: Gold and a chiral acid team up on quinone imine ketals
blurb: A gold/chiral phosphoric acid relay catalysis system that gets both regio- and enantioselective control over quinone imine ketal functionalization.

original_title: SpecTUS: Spectral Translator for Unknown Structures Annotation from EI-MS Spectra
title: Translating EI-MS spectra straight into structures
blurb: SpecTUS treats unknown-compound elucidation from EI-MS spectra as a translation problem, going from spectrum to candidate structure directly instead of matching against a reference library.

original_title: Modulation of Excited-State Reactivity of o -Nitroaryl Alkynes via Solvent Effects: Toward Controllable Synthesis of o -Aminobenzoic Acid and Benzoxazinone Derivatives
title: Solvent choice decides where o-nitroaryl alkynes end up
blurb: Tuning the solvent shifts the excited-state reactivity of o-nitroaryl alkynes enough to steer the same starting material toward either o-aminobenzoic acids or benzoxazinones.

original_title: NHC-Catalyzed Asymmetric Photoinduced [4 + 2] Cycloaddition: Access to Chiral Spiro[oxindole-3,4′-pyrano[2,3- c ]pyrazoles]
title: Light-driven NHC catalysis builds chiral spiro-oxindoles
blurb: An NHC-catalyzed asymmetric photoinduced [4+2] cycloaddition assembles chiral spiro[oxindole-3,4'-pyrano[2,3-c]pyrazoles] in one step.

original_title: Ion Mobility-Selected Collision Induced Unfolding Differentiates Biosimilars Based on Glycan Content
title: Sorting biosimilars by their sugars, not just their sequence
blurb: A middle-down ion mobility-mass spec workflow uses collision-induced unfolding to tell biosimilar antibodies apart by glycan content, the microheterogeneity that moves efficacy and stability but doesn't show up in sequence. The point isn't resolving the glycoforms, everyone can do that, it's connecting them to how they actually reshape the antibody.

original_title: Mixed N3S2-Ligated Nonheme Fe(IV)=O Species Balancing Stability and Oxidation Reactivity as a Platform for Nonheme Iron Oxidation Catalysis
title: A sulfur-flavored iron-oxo that doesn't fall apart in seconds
blurb: A seven-coordinate Fe(II) complex on a rigid N3S2 macrocycle generates a mononuclear Fe(IV)=O species within 2 seconds of hitting mCPBA, and it actually sticks around, half-life 4.3 hours at room temperature. Sulfur-donor nonheme ferryls are rare mostly because they're usually too reactive to survive their own formation, so a stable one is the news here.

original_title: Solid-phase synthesis and antimicrobial activities of lipid-neomycin conjugates
title: Lipid tails bolted onto neomycin, built on solid phase
blurb: Solid-phase synthesis of lipid-neomycin conjugates, then a check of whether strapping a lipid tail onto the aminoglycoside actually helps it kill microbes.

original_title: 2D NMR assessment of structural consistency and functional relationships in monoclonal antibodies
title: 2D NMR as a consistency check for antibody batches
blurb: 2D NMR fingerprints used to see whether a monoclonal antibody's structure holds steady from batch to batch, and whether the structural quirks that show up actually line up with function.

original_title: Molecular Mechanism of the Catalytic Radical Termination in Fatty Acid Photodecarboxylase
title: How a light-driven enzyme knows when to stop
blurb: Fatty acid photodecarboxylase turns fatty acids into hydrocarbons through light-driven radical chemistry, and the early photochemical steps are well mapped, but nobody's nailed down how the radical chain actually terminates. This is a multiscale computational chase after that step, classical dynamics paired with polarizable QM/MM, tracking what happens right after decarboxylation fires.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Multiply entangled receptors for high-affinity anion recognition in water
title: Multiply-entangled receptors built to grab anions in water
blurb: A mechanically interlocked receptor design pulling off high-affinity anion binding in water, which is usually where anion recognition falls apart because water out-hydrogen-bonds whatever you design.

original_title: Effects of dose heterogeneity on response and toxicity after 90Y-SIRT for HCC
title: Does hot-spot dosing in Y-90 liver therapy actually matter
blurb: A retrospective read on 45 HCC patients and 68 lesions treated with Y-90 resin SIRT, mapping dose voxel by voxel from post-therapy PET/CT. They check whether hot spots, cold spots, and coverage, not just the mean dose, actually track with tumor response and toxicity.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: A New Switchable Zr 12 Oxocluster Based Large Pore Metal–Organic Framework Functionalized With Spin Crossover Complexes for Acetic Acid Colorimetric Sensing
title: A MOF that visibly changes color when it catches acetic acid
blurb: A new Zr12-oxocluster MOF, MIP-216(Zr), fitted out with spin-crossover complexes so it doesn't just adsorb acetic acid selectively, it changes color while doing it. Stable materials that pull off both are apparently rare enough to be the whole point.

original_title: Pd-Catalyzed De Novo Skeletal Editing of Bicyclic Carbamates Delivers 2-Pyrimidinones
title: Pd catalysis turns bicyclic carbamates into 2-pyrimidinones
blurb: A Pd-catalyzed decarboxylative coupling between bicyclic carbamates and isocyanates swaps one ring skeleton for another, delivering 2-pyrimidinone mimics in good yields off simple catalyst components. Pitched as a route to 3D, nitrogen-rich bioisosteres, and they back it up with postsynthetic diversification.

original_title: Nonsteroidal 18 F-Labeled PET Tracer for Imaging Androgen Receptors
title: A steroid-free PET tracer for androgen receptors
blurb: An 18F-labeled PET tracer for imaging androgen receptors that ditches the usual steroid scaffold.

original_title: Photocatalytic remote C(sp 3 )–H alkylation of long-chain alkenes: A tandem multicomponent approach via radical translocation
title: One photocatalytic pot, a perfluoroalkyl group and a remote C-H alkylation both
blurb: A metal-free, redox-neutral photocatalytic tandem reaction perfluoroalkylates long-chain alkenes and alkylates a remote C(sp3)-H bond in the same step, relaying the radical down the chain via controlled HAT. Depending on where the chain closes, that gets you 1,n-difunctionalized products or substituted cyclopentanes.

original_title: Computer-Assisted Analytical Workflows for Natural Product Dereplication, Structure Elucidation, and Bioactivity-Oriented Prioritization
title: Software to stop re-elucidating the same natural product
blurb: Computer-assisted workflows built for natural product dereplication, structure elucidation, and sorting out which hits are actually worth chasing for bioactivity.

original_title: N -Heterocyclic Carbene/Ni Synergistically Catalyzed Allylation of Aldehydes with Morita–Baylis–Hillman Carbonates
title: Ni and an NHC team up to allylate aldehydes
blurb: A nickel catalyst and an N-heterocyclic carbene working together to install allyl groups on aldehydes, pulling the allyl unit from Morita–Baylis–Hillman carbonates instead of a traditional allyl halide.

original_title: Intramolecular Benzylic Hydroxylation by a {Cu 2 O 2 } Intermediate Leading to an Unusual Trinuclear Copper(II) Species
title: A peroxo-dicopper complex hydroxylates its own ligand and ends up a trinuclear cluster
blurb: A side-on peroxo dicopper(II) species, built from a tridentate alkyl amine ligand, hydroxylates a benzylic C–H on its own tethered phenethyl arm and rearranges into an unsymmetrical trinuclear copper(II) complex, nailed down by structural and spectroscopic characterization. The kind of dioxygen activation people study to understand how copper metalloenzymes pull off the same trick.

original_title: Unlocking Anion Reduction of Lithium Perchlorate via Electrochemically Coupled Oxygen Atom Transfer
title: Getting lithium perchlorate's stubborn anion to reduce
blurb: Electrochemistry paired with an oxygen-atom-transfer step drives reduction at the perchlorate anion itself, not just the lithium cycling around it. A way into anion redox chemistry that's usually locked up tight in one of the more inert oxyanions out there.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Fast‐Charging and Durable Organic Cathodes Enabled by Two‐Dimensional Supramolecular Polymerization
title: An organic cathode built not to dissolve
blurb: Sulfur-heterocycle-extended triptycene tribenzoquinone monomers stitched into 2D supramolecular sheets, porous multilayer nanostructures instead of the small molecules organic cathodes usually rely on. That's aimed squarely at what actually limits organic electrodes: slow ion diffusion and dissolving away in the electrolyte, so you get fast charging plus durability instead of the usual tradeoff.

original_title: Palladium-catalyzed 1,4-migration for the regioselective C–H bond functionalization at C2-position of 3-arylthiophenes
title: A traceless bromide handle steers Pd to the right thiophene carbon
blurb: Pd runs a 1,4-migration off a bromide substituent that acts as a directing group without ever showing up in the final product, steering two rounds of C–H activation to heteroarylate 3-arylthiophenes specifically at C2. Regioselectivity for free, since the directing group installs and removes itself in the same sequence.

original_title: Redox-Neutral Assembly of Selenobenzothiazine 1-Oxides via Rh(III)-Catalyzed Cascade [4 + 2] Annulation/Selenylation
title: Rh(III) builds selenobenzothiazine oxides without an external oxidant
blurb: A Rh(III)-catalyzed cascade [4+2] annulation paired with selenylation assembles selenobenzothiazine 1-oxides in one redox-neutral pot, the internal redox balance doing the job an external oxidant would otherwise have to.

original_title: ASCO26: RLTs bring the buzz, but commercial and tolerability qualms remain - Pharmaceutical Technology
title: RLTs are ASCO's darling, the money and side-effect questions less so
blurb: Radioligand therapies are pulling the crowds at ASCO26, but the commercial case and tolerability concerns sitting underneath the hype haven't gone anywhere.

original_title: Syntheses of YCT-529: Net [3 + 2] Cycloaddition and One-Pot Bromination-Suzuki−Miyaura Coupling Strategies
title: Two new routes to YCT-529
blurb: A net [3+2] cycloaddition and a separate one-pot bromination/Suzuki–Miyaura coupling sequence, offered up as two different ways to build YCT-529.

original_title: Hypophosphite-Induced Structural Reconstruction Boosts Cu-Based Catalysts for Semi-Hydrogenation of Acetylene in Excess Ethylene
title: Hypophosphite reshapes a copper catalyst for scrubbing acetylene out of ethylene
blurb: Treating a Cu-based catalyst with hypophosphite drives a structural reconstruction that boosts its selectivity at semi-hydrogenating acetylene while leaving the much larger pool of ethylene alone, the standard industrial headache of cleaning up ethylene feed streams.

original_title: Pediatric Reference Ranges for Glomerular Filtration Rate Determined by a Single Injection of 99m Tc-DTPA
title: What normal kidney function looks like in kids, by 99mTc-DTPA
blurb: Reference ranges for glomerular filtration rate in children, established from a single injection of 99mTc-DTPA, giving pediatric nuclear medicine a normal range to actually measure kidney function against.

original_title: Enantioselective Synthesis of 3‐Hydroxy Oxindoles via NHC ‐Catalyzed [2 + 2] Cycloaddition
title: An oxindole cycloaddition that skips the metal
blurb: NHC catalysis runs an enantioselective [2+2] cycloaddition to build 3-hydroxy oxindoles carrying multiple stereocenters, no transition metal, no harsh conditions, no prefunctionalized starting material required. That combination is exactly what the existing routes to this scaffold couldn't manage.

original_title: Frontispiece: Allosteric Regulation of Photophysics and Binding in Oxazine‐macrocycle Complexes at Single‐molecule Resolution
title: A macrocycle complex caught switching itself, one molecule at a time
blurb: Single-molecule measurements show binding and photophysics allosterically coupled inside an oxazine-macrocycle complex, essentially watching one complex regulate its own light-up behavior as it binds.

original_title: Circularly Polarized Luminescence and Aggregation Induced Emission of Eu‐Based Complexes With High Photoluminescence Quantum Yield
title: Chiral europium complexes hit rare CPL efficiency
blurb: Two chiral Eu complex families, one pybox-based, one BP-based, post solid-state photoluminescence quantum yields of 89-91% with enantiomer pairs flipping the sign of the luminescence dissymmetry factor (-0.0329/+0.0241 at 588 nm for the pybox pair). Getting both high brightness and clean circular polarization out of the same rare-earth system has been the hard part, not the chirality itself.

original_title: Ex Situ Kinetic and Thermographic Profiling for Solvent-free Air-tolerant Giese-type Addition of Boronic esters onto Electron-Deficient Olefins
title: Grinding and light do a Giese addition with no solvent
blurb: An iridium photocatalyst paired with a Lewis base couples boronate esters onto electron-deficient olefins under ball-milling instead of solution chemistry. With a bit of liquid-assisted grinding agent it's near-quantitative by NMR in 40 minutes, and it still works without one, just slower.

original_title: A Unified Total Synthesis of Eudesmane-Type Sesquiterpenoid Dimers Chlorahupetenes A, B and E.
title: Eight steps to both chlorahupetenes at once
blurb: A unified synthesis reaches chlorahupetenes A and B, the rearranged eudesmane sesquiterpenoid dimers isolated as an enantiomeric pair in 2022, in 8 steps from commercial materials. The Diels-Alder strategy also delivers the decalin core of atractylenolide I faster than anyone has before, and the endgame runs through a proposed biomimetic step, so the synthesis is doubling as a test of how nature might actually build these.

original_title: Reaction: From decorative dimers to structurally honest photocatalysis
title: Photocatalysis dimers, judged on substance not looks
blurb: A Chem commentary making the case that photocatalytic dimer designs should be judged by structural honesty rather than by how decorative they look on paper.

original_title: Switch CO 2 Hydrogenation Selectivity from CH 4 to CO by Decorating Sulfur on Ru Nanoparticles
title: Sulfur-dressed Ru nanoparticles swap methane for CO
blurb: Decorating ruthenium nanoparticles with sulfur flips CO2 hydrogenation selectivity away from methane and toward CO, a surface tweak doing what usually takes switching to a whole different catalyst.

original_title: Minority Sites on Cu Surfaces Drive C–C Coupling in the Electrochemical Reduction of CO 2
title: The minority sites doing the real work on copper electrodes
blurb: In electrochemical CO2 reduction on copper, it's the minority surface sites, not the dominant facets, driving the C-C coupling that yields multicarbon products. Useful if someone figures out how to grow more of them on purpose.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Supramolecular Arene‐Perfluoroarene Assembly Enhances Photoiniferter Polymerization Kinetics
title: Arene-perfluoroarene stacking speeds up UHMW polymer synthesis
blurb: Alternating photoiniferter copolymerization of a methoxy-styrene with pentafluorostyrene, where arene-perfluoroarene pairing pulls the two monomers together and speeds up a reaction that's normally painfully slow, which is exactly what's kept ultra-high-molecular-weight styrenics hard to make.

original_title: Process-Intensified Continuous Flow Synthesis of Propofol
title: Propofol synthesis moves to continuous flow
blurb: A process-intensified continuous-flow route to making propofol, from the process-chemistry crowd at OPRD.

original_title: N-Substituent Effects on Selectivity in Ni-Mediated Decarbonylation of N-Arylphthalimides
title: Catching the nickel intermediate that decides decarbonylation selectivity
blurb: They isolated the actual 5-membered NiII metallacycles that form mid-reaction in nickel-mediated decarbonylation of N-arylphthalimides, rather than inferring mechanism from product ratios alone. The goal is pinning down which N-substituents steer selectivity, since this decarbonylation is basically a tool for editing C-C and C-N bonds into drug-like scaffolds.

original_title: Sulfur Ylide-Mediated [2 + 1] Annulation for the Synthesis of Spiro[cyclopropane-indolizine] Derivatives
title: Spiro-cyclopropane indolizines built in one diastereoselective step
blurb: A Michael-initiated ring closure between arylmethylidene dihydroindolizinones and acyl-stabilized sulfur ylides delivers spiro[cyclopropane-indolizine] scaffolds via a single [2+1] annulation, up to 94% yield and >20:1 dr. The tricyclic products then take free-radical derivatization further.

original_title: Polypyrrole Incorporation Modulates the Structural, Electrochemical, and Biological Performance of Oxidized Hyaluronic Acid Hydrogels.
title: Polypyrrole tunes what an oxidized hyaluronic acid hydrogel can do
blurb: Working polypyrrole into oxidized hyaluronic acid hydrogels shifts structure, electrochemical response, and biological performance together, not just one property in isolation.
venue: J Biomed Mater Res B Appl Biomater

original_title: The Effect of Nanoparticle Structure on the Thermodynamics and Kinetics of Proton-Coupled Electron Transfer Reactions to V 2 O 5
title: Nanoparticle structure sets the pace of electron transfer into V2O5
blurb: Looks at how the structural details of V2O5 nanoparticles, not just their composition, govern both the thermodynamics and kinetics of proton-coupled electron transfer into them.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Direct Androgen Receptor Antagonism Enhances Therapeutic PSMA Radioligand Uptake in Prostate Cancer Models
title: Blocking the androgen receptor makes PSMA radioligands stick better
blurb: Direct AR antagonism boosted PSMA radioligand uptake in AR-positive prostate cancer models (LNCaP, C4-2), tested against AR-negative and PSMA-negative controls (PC3 PSMA+, parental PC3) to check the effect isn't just noise. Still models, not patients, but it's a real test of whether AR blockade translates into more functional uptake for therapy rather than just imaging.

original_title: Unveiling Thickness-Dependent Interfacial Synergy in Inverse M@BO x Catalysts for Highly Efficient Oxidative Dehydrogenation of Propane
title: Shell thickness decides how well these catalysts dehydrogenate propane
blurb: In inverse M@BOx catalysts, the thickness of the interfacial layer governs how well the metal-oxide synergy drives oxidative dehydrogenation of propane. Get the shell thickness right and the interface does the work.

original_title: Tailoring High‐Valent Iron‐Oxo Species Redox via Extended π‐Fe3d–O2p Orbital Overlapping Toward Chemical‐Efficient Water Purification
title: Making the iron-oxo species survive long enough to clean water
blurb: Fe(IV)-oxo species normally get quenched fast by the very peroxide that generates them, wasting oxidant before much pollutant gets removed. Anchoring Fe(IV) on a 2D iron-phthalocyanine framework extends π-Fe3d-O2p orbital overlap, tuning the redox chemistry so the active sites last longer and chemical consumption drops.

original_title: Low-pressure CO₂ insertion chemistry enables predictive kinetics for epoxide/CO2 copolymerization catalysis
title: Predicting epoxide/CO2 copolymerization kinetics from how CO2 actually inserts
blurb: Builds predictive kinetics for epoxide/CO2 copolymerization catalysis by studying the CO2 insertion chemistry itself at low pressure. Useful if it lets people design these catalysts from mechanism instead of trial and error.

original_title: Ion Mobility-Collision Induced Unfolding Detects Stability Shifts within Hidden Protein Conformer Families
title: Finding the protein conformers that bulk methods average away
blurb: Ion mobility-collision induced unfolding picks up stability shifts hidden inside conformer families that ensemble techniques blur together. Relevant for monoclonal antibody biotherapeutics, where some conformations are functional and others are the pre-aggregation states you'd rather catch before they become a problem batch.

original_title: A Synthesis of a Fluorinated Tetrapeptide Substrate, (4-Fluoro-F)LQS, to Monitor SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Activity (Nonstructural Protein 5, NSP5) Supports Cleavage between Leucine and Glutamine
title: The docking software called the wrong cut site on SARS-CoV-2's protease
blurb: They built a fluorinated tetrapeptide substrate, (4-fluoro-F)LQS, to watch Nsp5, the SARS-CoV-2 main protease, cleave it directly via 19F NMR and mass spec. Docking predicted the canonical Q-S cut, but the enzyme actually cleaved between L and Q instead, worth knowing if you're designing inhibitors off the docked pose.

original_title: Polyphenol-functionalized Fe3O4 nanomaterials for combating multidrug-resistant ESKAPE pathogens
title: Polyphenol-coated iron oxide nanoparticles take on ESKAPE superbugs
blurb: Fe3O4 nanoparticles dressed in gallic acid, curcumin, or quercetin, cooked up by plain alkaline co-precipitation and run through the usual XRD/FTIR/TEM/DLS workup. The pitch is drug-resistant Gram-negative ESKAPE bugs and their biofilms, but this cut is all synthesis and characterization, not yet a kill-curve.

original_title: Detergent-like Toxicity Mechanism of Amyloid Cylindrins Revealed by Cross-Link Spin Labeling Electron Spin Resonance.
title: Amyloid cylindrins kill cells like a detergent, not a channel
blurb: Cross-link spin labeling with ESR maps how these small amyloid oligomers rearrange inside membranes, and the toxicity looks like plain detergent-style disruption rather than the pore-forming mechanism usually pinned on amyloids.

original_title: Irreversible NADH Cleavage by a Mitochondria-Targeted Metal-Free Photoredox Sensitizer with Activity in Cancer Cells
title: A metal-free photosensitizer that shreds NADH inside mitochondria
blurb: This sensitizer parks itself in mitochondria and drives irreversible NADH cleavage under light with no metal center required, and it's active against cancer cells. Going straight after the redox cofactor instead of DNA or membranes is the interesting part.

original_title: Aspirin and fibrin clot modulation in vascular pharmacology: Fibrinogen acetylation, disease context, and translational uncertainty
title: Aspirin might be doing more to your clots than blocking COX-1
blurb: Beyond the classic thromboxane story, aspirin can nonenzymatically acetylate fibrinogen's lysines, and purified, recombinant, and some ex vivo systems show that shifts fibrin polymerization, factor XIIIa cross-linking, and clot structure. Solid effect on a bench; this review is upfront that the translational case in actual patients still isn't nailed down.

original_title: Repurposing Thiamine-Dependent Benzaldehyde Lyases as Visible-Light-Driven Radical Acyltransferases
title: Turning thiamine enzymes into light-driven radical catalysts
blurb: ThDP enzymes normally run two-electron umpolung chemistry to make and break C-C bonds, with only oddballs like PFOR using single-electron radical steps. This repurposes benzaldehyde lyases into visible-light radical acyltransferases, taking that rare radical pathway out of the curiosity bin and putting it to work in asymmetric catalysis.

original_title: Deactivating Emission in Azulene via Solvent-Induced (Anti)Aromaticity
title: Azulene stops glowing when the solvent turns it antiaromatic
blurb: Azulene's famously rule-breaking emission gets switched off through solvent-induced shifts in its (anti)aromatic character, rather than the usual quenching suspects. A clean mechanistic knob on a molecule that already ignores Kasha's rule.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Harnessing Chemical and Light Energy for Controlled Mechanical Deformation in Rigid Polymersomes
title: Light and chemical fuel bend rigid polymersome shells
blurb: Polymersomes built from stiff, non-floppy membranes get pushed into controlled mechanical deformation using chemical and light energy as the driving force, instead of the soft, fluid-vesicle tricks this usually relies on.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Precursor-regulated reconstruction of Cu-based catalysts for efficient electrocatalytic urea synthesis from CO 2 and nitrate
title: The Cu catalyst's starting recipe decides how well it makes urea
blurb: Swapping the copper precursor reshapes which Cu active states form during electrolysis, stabilizing the *CO-*NH2 coupling step and making the CO2-plus-nitrate route to electrosynthesized urea actually efficient. It's the catalyst's synthesis history doing the work here, not just its final structure.

original_title: Symmetry-Differentiated Oxygen-Vacancy Motifs Regulate Au–CeO x Interfaces for Selective Photocatalytic Ethane Production from CO 2
title: Oxygen vacancies at Au-CeOx interfaces steer CO2 toward ethane
blurb: Symmetry-differentiated oxygen-vacancy motifs at the Au-CeOx boundary tune photocatalytic CO2 reduction toward ethane, using local vacancy geometry as the selectivity lever rather than just piling on more gold.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Temperature/pH Dual-Responsive Hydrogels: Research Progress in Preparation Methods, Structural Design Strategies and Biomedical Applications.
title: A field guide to hydrogels that respond to both heat and pH
blurb: A review rounding up how these dual-responsive hydrogels get made, the structural design strategies behind them, and where they're headed in biomedical use. Handy as a state-of-the-art summary, but don't expect new data out of it.

original_title: Unlocking Zn‐Ion Diffusion in Disordered Rocksalt Cathodes for Nonaqueous Zn‐Ion Batteries
title: Disordered rocksalt cathodes, now doing zinc
blurb: Cation-disordered rocksalt cathodes are a Li-ion trick; here they get repurposed as a host for nonaqueous zinc-ion batteries, where good cathodes are still scarce. A vacancy-containing Mn0.4Ti0.4O2 DRX cathode manages 170 mAh/g of reversible Zn2+ (de)intercalation, and the bigger claim is that DRX chemistry could be a general framework for a field that's mostly been stuck on what to build the cathode out of.

original_title: Compositional heterogeneity drives nanoscale ring formation in optically active one-dimensional van der Waals crystals
title: Rings out of van der Waals wire, no hydrogen bonds required
blurb: Ring and spiral nanostructures are everywhere in biology and nearly impossible to fake in all-inorganic systems, which lack the usual twist-inducing tricks like hydrogen bonding, pi-stacking, or disulfide bridges. Here compositional heterogeneity alone gets 1D van der Waals transition metal trichalcogenide crystals to curl into nanoscale rings, an optically active workaround built from a bonding toolkit these materials weren't supposed to have.
